svg.title: Long-File Chunking & Subprocess Isolation in PARSE
svg.desc: A 3-hour PARSE audio timeline split into 18 ten-minute chunks, processed inside protected subprocess boxes, then merged back onto the original timeline with chunk progress indicators.
svg.text: Long-File Chunking & Subprocess Isolation in PARSE
3-hour example: full-file STT / full-mode ORTH use 18 adjacent 10-minute chunks; risky model work runs in child processes.
1 · Split the original audio timeline into bounded work units
split_audio_duration(total_seconds, chunk_seconds): adjacent spans, original speaker/audio unchanged
2 · Process each chunk behind a subprocess isolation boundary
Each box is a protected child process: model/provider loads inside the child; crash/OOM/timeout is contained and serialized.
3 · Merge successful chunks back onto the original time ruler
Result contract: chunks[] records diagnostics; merged STT cache / ORTH tiers remain a flat whole-speaker timeline.
Grounded in docs/architecture/chunking.md, python/workers/audio_chunking.py, python/server_routes/media.py, annotate.py, and jobs.py.
